title: trifolium howellii
intro: Trifolium howellii, also known as Howell's clover or Howell's trefoil, is a plant species in the legume family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Trifolium howellii include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and act as an antimicrobial agent. Trifolium howellii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, and it has been used by indigenous communities for centuries to treat a variety of ailments.
